items.title: US H-1B visa applicants to be vetted over "censorship" jobs | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>A US State Department memo directs officials to deny visas to H-1B applicants involved in online "censorship," targeting those working in jobs such as content moderation or fact-checking.

items.title: US lawmakers are questioning US Defense Secretary’s conduct in office | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Democratic and some Republican lawmakers in the US are calling for Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, a former TV-host — who now calls himself Secretary of War — to be fired after multiple scandals. The controversial official is under scrutiny after his latest orders to strike alleged drug-trafficking boats in the Caribbean which has been labeled as extrajudicial killings by some.

0:00 Pete Hegseth faces pressure  
3:29 Mark Cancian, Retired Colonel and senior adviser with the Center for Strategic and International Studies

items.title: The German chancellor begins his Middle East trip | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Merz's first stop is Jordan's capital Amman for a meeting with King Abdullah. He'll then travel on to Israel to meet Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Sunday. It's his first trip to both countries as chancellor. He will hold talks with regional leaders focused on consolidating the ceasefire in Gaza and seek to strengthen ties between Germany and Israel which were strained by the war in Gaza.

items.title: FIFA presents new Peace Prize to Trump at draw for the 2026 Men’s Soccer World Cup | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Washington has hosted the draw for next summer's football World Cup. A record 48 nations are competing in the tournament, which is being jointly hosted by the US, Mexico and Canada.
The leaders of those three nations — US President Donald Trump, Mexico's Claudia Sheinbaum, and Canada's Mark Carney — revealed the make-up of the 12 groupings.

0:00 12 groups were drawn for the 2026 Men’s Soccer World Cup on Friday
0:53 Constantin Eckner, Sports analyst and journalist

items.title: EU Foreign Policy Chief Kaja Kallas reacts to US national security strategy paper | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>EU's Foreign Policy Chief Kaja Kallas has downplayed a new US strategy paper critical of Europe. Published on Friday, the document called Europe over-regulated and facing "civilizational erasure" because of immigration.
Speaking at the Doha Forum in Qatar, Kallas said the US remained Europe's biggest ally, despite policy differences. Her comments are in contrast to Germany's foreign minister Johann Wadepful, who rejected what he called, "outside advice."

0:00 Kaja Kallas, High Representative of the European Union for Foreign Affairs and Security Policy reacts to new US national security strategy

1:09 Laurel Rapp, Director of the US and North America Program at the Chatham House think tank


#usa #europe #security

items.title: One of India's biggest airlines cancels more than 1,000 flights due to new crew rest rules | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>After major disruptions at many Indian airports, India's aviation authority has rolled back regulations mandating longer rest periods for pilots and crews, The country's biggest airline IndiGo says it cancelled more than 1,000 flights, citing "unforeseen operational challenges". The new rules came into effect last month to give pilots more rest and boost passenger safety.  

0:00 Major disruptions at airports across India
2:37 Bhavya Velani, Aviation Expert and CEO of Aviation A 2 Z

items.title: How was the corruption scandal perceived in the US? | To the Point
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Are US–Russia talks about Ukraine really about peace—or big business? Donald Trump didn’t just send diplomats. He sent his son-in-law Jared Kushner and real estate mogul Steve Witkoff to Moscow. Behind the so-called peace plan, insiders claim major economic deals with Vladimir Putin are on the table. Could this come at Ukraine’s expense and threaten Europe’s security? 

You can watch the full episode of the podcast here: https://youtu.be/TPaBGtAccog

items.title: Why hasn't Indonesia declared a state of emergency yet? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Indonesians are bracing for more heavy rains after floods and landslides devastated regions in the north and west. More than 880 people have been confirmed dead and hundreds are missing. A chain of tropical storms hit South and Southeast Asia last week. More heavy rain is expected in the next 24 hours. DW's Georg Matthes has this report from North Sumatra.

Sri Lanka is also bracing for more devastation after it was hit by a cyclone last week. Forecasters are warning of fresh rains and landslides. More than 600 are dead and another 200 people are missing. Around two million people have been affected by floods and landslides. Tens of thousands of homes have been damaged. The government has announced a compensation package to help people rebuild. 

00:00 Indonesia's military is stepping up relief efforts in the worst-hit areas
02:57 DW Correspondent Cinta Zanidya in North Sumatra
06:28 Sri Lanka threatened by more rain and landslides 
06:57 Amal Jayasinghe, Sri Lanka Bureau Chief for the AFP news agency

items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>US President Trump has been awarded the FIFA Peace Prize — an unusual break from tradition for the football-focused organization. Critics say: "FIFA is dragging itself into becoming part of a public relations tool to normalize an increasingly authoritarian US government."

items.title: War in Donbas: The last maternity ward | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>In the war-torn Donbas region in eastern Ukraine, a single maternity ward remains the only hope for mothers-to-be. Around 40 babies are delivered here each month - despite the lack in resources and the strain on the staff. Why are young families staying in the region? And why are some even choosing to return? Our report shows the reality behind the front line: New life and hope in a life-threatening and insecure situation.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Are South African men being lured into fighting Russia's war in Ukraine? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>South Africa's Zuma family is at the center of a scandal where the former president Jacob Zuma’s daughter is accused of tricking a group of men into joining Russia’s war in Ukraine. South African police are investigating the claims.

President Cyril Ramaphosa’s office confirmed it received distress calls from 17 South African men who say they were sent to the frontline in Ukraine without their consent. In a separate case, five others have been arrested on suspicion they were planning to travel to Russia as mercenaries, this is illegal under South African law.

00:00 Are South African men being lured into fighting Russia's war in Ukraine?
00:49 DW Correspondent Dianne Hawker reports from Johannesburg
05:13 Sanusha Naidu, Institute For Global Dialogue 

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: What does Trump's National Security Strategy signal about Washington’s priorities in Asia? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>US President Donald Trump has unveiled a new document outlining his vision for US foreign policy, and how to deal with the threat of conflict with China. The National Security Strategy released on Friday keeps the defense of Taiwan a priority, but urges Indo-Pacific allies to share more of the burden.

The document says: "Preventing conflict requires a vigilant posture in the Indo-Pacific, a renewed defense industrial base, greater military investment from ourselves and from allies and partners, and winning the economic and technological competition over the long term."

00:00 US unveils new strategy to prevent China-Taiwan conflict - What does the document say? 
00:39 Huong Le Thu is Deputy Director Asia, International Crisis Group. She spoke to DW about about Washington's priorities in Asia.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/
